12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }
25→
26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
28→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
29→}
30→
31→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)

  3→// See RFC 5321, 4.5.3.1.3.

  6→
  7→// See RFC 5321, 4.5.3.1.1.
  8→internal const val MAXIMUM_LOCAL_PART_LENGTH = 64
  9→
 10→/**
 11→ * Represents an email address.
 12→ *
 13→ * This class currently doesn't support internationalized domain names (RFC 5891) or non-ASCII local parts (RFC 6532).
 14→ */
 15→class EmailAddress internal constructor(
 16→    val localPart: String,
 17→    val domain: EmailDomain,
 18→) {
 19→    val encodedLocalPart: String = if (localPart.isDotString) localPart else quoteString(localPart)
 20→
 21→    val warnings: Set<Warning>
 22→
 23→    init {
 24→        warnings = buildSet {
 25→            if (localPart.length > MAXIMUM_LOCAL_PART_LENGTH) {
 26→                add(Warning.LocalPartExceedsLengthLimit)
 27→            }
 28→
 29→            if (address.length > MAXIMUM_EMAIL_ADDRESS_LENGTH) {
 30→                add(Warning.EmailAddressExceedsLengthLimit)
 31→            }
 32→
 33→            if (localPart.isEmpty()) {
 34→                add(Warning.EmptyLocalPart)
 35→            }
 36→
 37→            if (!localPart.isDotString) {
 38→                add(Warning.QuotedStringInLocalPart)
 39→            }
 40→        }
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    val address: String
 44→        get() = "$encodedLocalPart@$domain"
 45→
 46→    val normalizedAddress: String
 47→        get() = "$encodedLocalPart@${domain.normalized}"
 48→
 49→    override fun toString(): String {
 50→        return address
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 54→        if (this === other) return true
 55→        if (other == null || this::class != other::class) return false
 56→
 57→        other as EmailAddress
 58→
 59→        if (localPart != other.localPart) return false
 60→        return domain == other.domain
 61→    }
 62→
 63→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 64→        var result = localPart.hashCode()
 65→        result = 31 * result + domain.hashCode()
 66→        return result
 67→    }

 69→    private fun quoteString(input: String): String {
 70→        return buildString {
 71→            append(DQUOTE)
 72→            for (character in input) {

 74→                    append(BACKSLASH)

 76→                append(character)

1→package app.k9mail.core.common.mail
 2→
 3→/**
 4→ * Configuration to control the behavior when parsing an email address into [EmailAddress].
 5→ *
 6→ * @param isLocalPartLengthCheckEnabled When this is `true` the length of the local part is checked to make sure it
 7→ * doesn't exceed the specified limit (see RFC 5321, 4.5.3.1.1.).
 8→ *
 9→ * @param isEmailAddressLengthCheckEnabled When this is `true` the length of the whole email address is checked to make
10→ * sure it doesn't exceed the specified limit (see RFC 5321, 4.5.3.1.3.; The maximum length of 'Path' indirectly limits
11→ * the length of 'Mailbox').
12→ *
13→ * @param isQuotedLocalPartAllowed When this is `true`, the parsing step allows email addresses with a local part
14→ * encoded as quoted string, e.g. `"foo bar"@domain.example`. Otherwise, the parser will throw an
15→ * [EmailAddressParserException] as soon as a quoted string is encountered.
16→ * Quoted strings in local parts are not widely used. It's recommended to disallow them whenever possible.
17→ *

 6→/**
 7→ * Used to parse name & email address pairs entered by the user.
 8→ *
 9→ * TODO: Build a custom implementation that can deal with typical inputs from users who are not familiar with the
10→ *  RFC 5322 address-list syntax. See (ignored) tests in `UserInputEmailAddressParserTest`.
11→ */
12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }
25→
26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
28→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
29→}

14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }
25→
26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
28→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
29→}
30→
31→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)

 6→/**

12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)

18→                when {

20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }

26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
